data.title: Hick's Law
data.description: Adobe applies Hick's Law by simplifying user choices to enhance decision-making speed.

data.title: Fitts' Law
data.description: Interface elements are designed to be easily selectable to streamline user interactions.

data.title: Miller's Law
data.description: Design principles accommodate the limitation of 7±2 items in human memory.

data.title: Jakob's Law
data.description: Consistency with user expectations is maintained to improve usability.

data.title: Aesthetic-Usability Effect
data.description: The interface design balances aesthetics with usability to enhance user attraction and satisfaction.
